title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Gate Lens: Dynamic Reachability
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates dynamic reachability increment in a dead-code and dependency audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Account for plugins, reflection, generated entry points, and framework conventions.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Dynamic Reachability evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-dynamic-reachability-13dfdn: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dynamic Reachability into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-dynamic-reachability-13dfdn:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the Dynamic Reachability model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Dynamic Reachability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Account for plugins, reflection, generated entry points, and framework conventions.
